title: Conversion and Hierarchy
description: Understand the rules for converting leads and the account hierarchy logic for different customer scenarios.

sections.title: The Critical Conversion Step
sections.content:
When a lead is qualified, it converts into the account hierarchy. This is critical because conversion determines account structure, parent/child relationships, and which account owns the opportunity.

Conversion isn't one-to-one. It follows specific rules based on whether this prospect is net-new or already exists in your system.

Get this wrong and you'll have duplicates, misattributed revenue, and reporting nightmares. Get it right and your entire system is accurate.

sections.title: The Four Conversion Scenarios
sections.content:
Scenario 1: Net-New Prospect
Company doesn't exist in Salesforce. Conversion creates:
• New Parent Account (represents entire enterprise)
• New Entity Child Account (represents specific business unit)
• New Contact record linked to Parent
• Setup ready for opportunity creation

Scenario 2: Existing Company, No Entity Child
Parent Account exists (you already work with another division), but no Entity Child for this prospect's business unit. Conversion:
• Links to existing Parent Account
• Creates new Entity Child Account
• Creates Contact record
• Both old and new relationships properly represented

Scenario 3: Existing Company, Existing Entity Child
Parent Account and Entity Child both exist. Prospect is in the same business unit (new contact or repeat contact). Conversion:
• Links to existing Parent and Entity Child
• Creates new Contact or links existing Contact
• No new accounts created
• Lead becomes part of existing hierarchy

Scenario 4: Partner-Led Account
Partner organization owns the primary relationship; NxN is the underlying provider. Conversion:
• Creates Partner/Reseller relationship structure
• Tracks NxN through Partner status or flag
• Or creates Partner Parent Account with NxN Entity Child beneath
• Different hierarchy logic than standard deals

sections.title: Account Hierarchy Structure
sections.content:
The account hierarchy supports complex organizations:

Parent Account
Represents the entire enterprise or holding company. Highest level. If NxN works with multiple divisions of the same company, they share the same Parent Account.

Entity Child Account
Represents the specific business unit, subsidiary, or delivery entity consuming NxN services. This is the account where opportunities are created. Multiple Entity Child Accounts can exist under one Parent (if you serve multiple divisions).

Additional Child Accounts (optional)
Some orgs have extra levels: departments, regional offices, project entities. Exist below Entity Child level.

Contact Association
Contacts link to Parent Account and are associated with one or more Entity Child Accounts through roles or relationship fields.

sections.title: How the System Matches
sections.content:
When converting a lead, the system determines the correct hierarchy using:

1. Company Name Matching — Fuzzy matching logic against existing Parent Accounts. Similar names flagged for review. Exact matches auto-linked.

2. Company Domain/Website — Email domain or website match confirms account identity.

3. Manual Override — If automated matching is uncertain, a human reviewer (CRM/Governance or Sales) manually selects the correct Parent or creates new.

4. Entity Child Selection — Once Parent is confirmed, system checks for existing Entity Child for that business unit. If exists, converts to it. If not, creates new.

Take time to verify correct Parent and Entity Child during conversion. Accuracy compounds throughout the customer lifecycle.

sections.title: Why This Matters
sections.content:
Correct conversion ensures:

✓ Opportunities created on right account → revenue attributed correctly
✓ Account managers see all parent company activity across divisions
✓ Duplicate accounts prevented → database stays clean
✓ Reporting reflects true customer base and pipeline

Incorrect conversion leads to:
✗ Duplicates
✗ Misattributed revenue
✗ Confused reporting
✗ Account manager frustration

Conversion is where accuracy is built or broken.

keyTakeaways: Four conversion scenarios determine account structure: Net-New, Existing with No Entity Child, Existing with Entity Child, Partner-Led
Conversion decisions directly impact revenue attribution and must be verified using company name matching and manual review when needed
Correct hierarchy prevents duplicates, misattributed revenue, and reporting confusion
